steps.title: Identify what the y-intercept represents
steps.content: The $y$-intercept is the point where a graph crosses the $y$-axis. This always occurs at $x = 0$.

steps.title: Read the y-coordinate directly from the graph
steps.content: At $x = 0$ on the graph, the curve intersects the $y$-axis at the point shown. Reading the $y$-coordinate at this intersection, we find the curve passes through $y = -3$. The $y$-coordinate of the $y$-intercept is -3.
